feed.title: Accessibility Wars - quickness
feed.subtitle: Accessible Builds for the Guild Wars 2 Community

entry.title: Staff Quickeye [qDPS]
entry.content:
The purpose of writing this guide is to provide a very low intensity method to play PvE deadeye while providing quickness. This build is low cognition, has low physical complexity, and has very few repetitions. This build is functional in both open world and instanced content. This build also provides abundance of Might and Fury boons that support healers that are lacking in effort.

Since Weakening Whirl (Staff 2) does the most damage per point of Initiative, you want to use it as much as possible over all of your other skills. You may press and hold this skill to reduce the number of button presses (APM).

Gearing and Consumables

Equipment

  • Full Berserker's up to the crit cap.
  • Dragon’s if desired as the build is heavily overcapped on precision
  • Dual Pistols

Runes

  •  Superior Rune of the Scholar OR
  •  Superior Rune of the Dragonhunter

Sigils

  •  Superior Sigil of Force
  •  Superior Sigil of Impact
  •  Superior Sigil of Air(Budget Alternative)

Relic

  •  Relic of the Thief

Food

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Plate of Spicy Moa Wings

Utility

  •  Superior Sharpening Stone(Power)

Build

Quickness DPS

[&DQUjGxw9Oj8MAQwBIBdYATMBMwGsFlYBKAAoAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACNgBZAAA=]
  • Note: Using Improvisation increases the quickness uptime and makes the build easier to play at a cost of slightly reduced DPS
  • You should prioritise quickness uptime instead of DPS as this affects the entire subgroup.
  • It is okay to overcap on precision due to Practiced Tolerance

Rotation and Utilities

Improvisation, One in the Chamber and Fire for Effect paired with Mercy provides a high amount of Quickness right at the start of the fight. For quickness, you need to use Steal Time. Each steal provides two charges.

DPS Rotation

Precasts - Thieves Guild (use on refresh) and Hide in Shadows(optional)

  1. Start the fight with Deadeye's Mark (steal)
  2. Use Staff 2 for damage and fill with autos.
  3. Mercy provides extra initiative which is used for extra quickness.

Quickness Rotation

  1. For the quickness, you need to use Steal Time. Deadeye's Mark provides one charge.
  2. Mercy as a cantrip provides 1 charge of Steal Time and resets Deadeye's Mark.
  3. Your other cantrip(s) also provide 1 charge of Steal Time
  4. You may reserve Steal Time at full malice to gain stealth/Revealed and a damage boost through Revealed Training
  5. If you mess up your rotation, your group will lose quickness, for example
    • using Mercy when Deadeye's Mark is about to refresh
    • using Deadeye's Mark before using the charge from Mercy
    • being split up from your group due to mechanics
  6. Consider taking improvisation if you find quickness difficult to upkeep.

Quickness Rotation with Improvisation

  1. Quickness Rotation
    • Steal Time 1st charge -> Weakening Whirl (until malice is filled)
    • Steal Time 2nd charge -> Weakening Whirl (until initiative is dry)
    • Fill with Autos until Mercy is ready (Step 3)
  2. Mercy -> Steal Time -> Thieves Guild -> Deadeye's Mark
  3. Quickness Rotation (Repeat Step 3 -> Step 2 indefinitely)
    • Steal Time 1st charge -> Weakening Whirl (until malice is filled)
    • Steal Time 2nd charge -> Weakening Whirl (until initiative is dry)
    • Fill with autos until Mercy is ready
  • Using stolen skills at max malice will give stealth.
  • Exiting Stealth will give revealed status which increases DPS through Revealed Training
  • Note: Using Assassin's Signet and Shadow Flare during revealed can increase DPS. For simplicity, place this after the 2nd charge of Steal Time.
  • Note: Using Shadow Flare will give an extra charge of Steal Time which can trigger revealed status again.

Crowd Control

  1. Malicious Hook Strike (Stealth Attack)
  2. Helmet Breaker (Staff 3 Flip)
  3. Basilisk Venom (Elite Swap)

Sustainability for Solo/OW

You may equip the following for extra sustainability:

  •  Relic of Zakiros
  • Signet of Malice
  • Invigorating Precision

Latest patch changes

Deadeye's Mark has no cast time and no longer interrupts your rotation. It now provides a shadowstep for melee builds.

entry.title: The Blast and the Quickness [pDPS]
entry.content:
Introduction

This is build has no kits and provides reliable DPS and an excessive amount of quickness with large amounts of superspeed.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Pistol Berserker's Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Dragon's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Dragon's
Ring Dragon's
Relic Claw
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Dragon's
Amulet Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
Accessory Dragon's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Dragon's
Ring Dragon's
Relic Claw

Consumables:

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQMmHwY3Ky0qD4QAJgEmAa4SAAAnEwAAiQGJAQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWgA2AFUAAA==]

GW2Skills Link

Notes

Granting Quickness and Superspeed

You give quickness through the trait Kinetic Accelerators by performing blast, leap or whirl combos when a field is active. Your field skills are

  1. Blast Gyro
  2. Function Gyro
  3. Spare Capacitor (no superspeed)
  • Pop your kit fields when lacking before using combos.
  • Your field gyros provide superspeed but Spare Capacitor does not. If you use Bypass Coating with Spare Capacitor, it will increase your superspeed uptime and hence increase your DPS.
  • Essentially if you don't want to memorise rotations, if you sweep from right to left (98754321)
  • If you mashed all your field skills and run out of them, you can create one using Elite Mortar Kit, although this is almost never necessary.
  • Other elite skills can provide additional utility/defiance break if you do not require the Elite Mortar Kit.
  • You can also use Function Gyro if you have allies that have been downed, and this will summon up to 3 seperate Gyros, which will automatically go revive allies nearby.

DPS Rotation

  1. Start this after providing quickness
  2. Blowtorch
  3. Refraction Cutter
  4. Auto-attack

Crowd Control

CC skills

  1. Throw Mine (used in DPS rotation)
  2. Static Shock (AED Toolbelt - backup)
  3. Spare Capacitor (CC + Lightning Field)
  4. Radiant Arc (Lightning Field Leap Combo)
  5. Supply Crate(Optional Elite)

Benchmark

The estimated DPS is provided without any infusions.

entry.title: Venom [qDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This build is simplified to use a fixed, consistent and easy-to-remember rotation. Condition damage and eight-legged pets are empowered through the trait Arachnophobia. This build however still performs decently and allows one to comfortably learn the class mechanics with fewer APMs while providing very good and steady quickness uptime.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Ritualist's
Amulet Ritualist's
Accessory Ritualist's
Accessory Ritualist's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Ritualist's
Ring Ritualist's
Relic Fractal
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Ritualist's Malice
Torch Ritualist's Doom
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Axe Ritualist's Malice
Dagger Ritualist's Geomancy

Consumables

  •  Salsa-Topped Veggie Flatbread
  •  Magnanimous Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQQeHSE3SB95AHkAvwClAb0AAAC6ABsADxvtAAYkKwEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADBQAvAGYAAA==]
Juvenile Carrion Devourer
Juvenile Black Widow Spider

Rotation

General Rotation

The entirety of quickness Untamed rotation can be summarised into two steps.

  1. Opener (optional)
  2. Quickness Rotation
  3. DPS Rotation
  4. Repeat

Precast

  1. Sharpening Stone

Opener

The opener can give a very good burst, especially when the boss has different phases.

  1. Unleash Pet
  2. All your Utility Skills (7/8/9)
  3. 3 pet skills (can be cast simultaneously)
    • Venomous Outburst (Essential Unleashed Pet F1)
    • Rending Vines (Optional F2)
    • Enveloping Haze (Optional F3)
  4. Unleash Ranger
  • If you skip the opener, you just have to use Unleash Ranger to unlock Unleashed Ambush to proceed with the quickness rotation.
  • The 3 pet skills have no cast time but venomous outburst is your biggest contributor. The other 2 may be skipped to lower your APM if they slow your rotation. The damage difference is negligible.
  • If you use a 2KRO/3KRO keyboard, your simultaneous cast of pet skills may result in input failure. Most gaming keyboards are 6KRO, allowing all the buttons to be pressed at the same time. Alternatively, can just use F2.

Quickness Rotation

This build starts off with a very simple quickness rotation through the trait Unleashed Power.

  1. Unleashed Ambush
    • Neurotoxin Burst OR
    • Sundering Volley
  2. 🔃 Weapon Swap
  3. Unleashed Ambush (other weapon)
  4. Continue with DPS Rotation

DPS Rotation - L to R (A/D) R to L (D/T)

After using your 2nd Unleash Ambush -> use the skills from left to right for Axe/Dagger and from right to left for Dagger/Torch. The first skill you use will be recharged faster. This skill is bolded in the rotation and should be cast twice before Unleash Pet and reentering the opener. This will maximise the effect of Quick Draw. This is bolded in the rotation below.

  • Dagger/Torch 5432 then 5
    1. Skill 5
    2. Skill 4
    3. Skill 3
    4. Skill 2
    5. Skill 5
    6. Pet F2 (if available)
    7. Continue with Opener (see above)
  • Axe/Dagger 2345 then 2
    1. Skill 2
    2. Skill 3
    3. Skill 4
    4. Skill 5
    5. Skill 2
    6. Pet F2 (if available)
    7. Continue with Opener (see above)
  • Your pet skills don't interrupt your rotation and you may use them as desired.
  • Any condition based spider or devourer pet is preferred due to Arachnophobia
  • If you use Unleash Ranger before your 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes, your Unleashed Ambush will not be ready.
  • Using Unleash Ranger too early will lead to a large DPS loss. Using it late will lead to a small DPS loss and maintain quickness.

Crowd Control Skills

  1. Tail Lash (F1)
  2. Exploding Spores (Utility Alternative)
  3. Spike Trap (Utility Alternative)
  4. Bring other pets if you need more

Benchmark

This bench was done with ascended food, standard utiltiies and without infusions.

entry.title: Whirling Bunny Thumper [qDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This build is simplified to activate the  Relic of the Claw to reduce relic downtime on burst windows on a fixed, consistent and easy-to-remember rotation. This provides steady quickness for the group and decent sustained CC. There is a relatively a large amount of button downtime filled with auto attacks. This build however still performs decently and allows one to comfortably learn the class mechanics with fewer APMs while providing very good and steady quickness uptime.

There are two channeled skills that will destroy your build when cancelled. These skills are positioned in the rotation where they are less likely to be cancelled (because the other skills are on cooldown).

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Claw
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Hammer Berserker's Air
Hydromancy
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Air
Axe Berserker's Hydromancy

Consumables

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQQgHx45SBd5AHkAARsAAKUBAACsAQAADxsAAEAQARMAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADMwBaAAUAAA==]
Juvenile Black Moa
Juvenile Wallow

Rotation

Start in Unleash Pet using Sword/Axe

General Rotation

The entirety of quickness Untamed rotation can be summarised into two steps.

  1. Opener (optional)
  2. Quickness Rotation
  3. DPS Rotation

Opener

The opener can give a very good burst, especially when the boss has different phases.

  1. Unleash Pet
  2. "Sic 'Em!"
  3. 3 pet skills (can be cast simultaneously)
    • Venomous Outburst
    • Rending Vines
    • Enveloping Haze
  4. Unleash Ranger
  • If you skip the opener, you have to use Unleash Ranger to unlock Unleash Ambush to give quickness.
  • Starting the opener with sword will trigger  Relic of the Claw right at the beginning which is a significant initial DPS burst.
  • If you use a 2KRO/3KRO keyboard, your simultaneous cast of pet skills may result in input failure. Most gaming keyboards are 6KRO, allowing all the buttons to be pressed at the same time. Alternatively, you press the three pet skills one by one as fast as you can.

Quickness Rotation

This build starts off with a very simple quickness rotation through the trait Unleashed Power.

  1. Unleashed Ambush
    • Relentless Whirl OR
    • Deft Strike
  2. 🔃 Weapon Swap
  3. Unleashed Ambush (other weapon)
  4. Continue with DPS Rotation

DPS Rotation (2/3/4/5/7/8)

After using your 2nd Unleash Ambush -> Autos about to start/started

  1. Weapon 2
    • Unleashed Wild Swing OR Pounce
  2. Weapon 3
    • Unleashed Overbearing Smash OR Serpent's Strike
  3. Weapon 4
    • Unleashed Savage Shock Wave OR Path of Scars
  4. Weapon 5
    • Unleashed Thump OR Whirling Defense
  5. Utilities 7/8 if available +/- Pet skill (F3)
    • Exploding Spores OR "Sic 'Em!"
    • Undead Plague
  6. Optional Weapon 2 on refresh
  7. Autos (till 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes)
  8. Unleash Pet -> Unleash Ranger
  9. Continue with Quickness Rotation
  • Use "Sic 'Em!" on cooldown or during a comfortable window when you are not pressing any skills.
  • Your pet skills don't interrupt your rotation and you may use them as desired. Any power based pet is viable.
  • Try not to cancel Relentless Whirl or Whirling Defense.
  • You may wish to introduce a slight delay after casting Relentless Whirl
  • The sequence of button presses are similar on both weapons.
  • If you are slow on your rotation, you can skip Weapon 3.
  • If you use Unleash Ranger before your 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes, your Unleashed Ambush will not be ready.
  • Dragging your feet on using ambush will lead to a small 1-2k DPS loss. Swapping Unleash Mode too early will lead to a higher DPS loss.

Relic of the Claw Uptime

< Relic of the Claw is triggered by your CC skills. These are littered between your important skill casts except during the end of the hammer phase. If you have followed the DPS rotation above Exploding Spores should be available towards the later part of your hammer DPS rotation.

If you started off with the wrong weapon, then remember to reserve Exploding Spores for hammer.

Crowd Control Skills

  1. Deft Strike before Relentless Whirl
  2. Path of Scars before Whirling Defense
  3. Exploding Spores during hammer rotation
  4. Your pet F3 skills deal CC through the trait Beastly Warden

Benchmark

This bench was done with ascended food, standard utiltiies and without infusions.

entry.title: Rifle and Roses [qHeal]
entry.content:
The Troubadour has an easy design such that when you randomly mash all the buttons, you'd provide all the right boons and an excessive amount of healing. This build has selected utilities for minimal cognitive complexity, such that the hardest part of playing this build is enduring the out-of-sync music throughout the fight.

While this guide assumes no weapon swap is needed, if you take an off-tank role, you may consider swapping rifle to other alternatives.

Gearing and Consumables

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
Harrier's Rebirth
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Rifle Harrier's Paralyzation
Transference
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Harrier's
Amulet Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Harrier's
Ring Harrier's
Relic Febe

This is a low toughness set-up.

Alternative

Weapon swap is optional:

Scepter and shield for tanking

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Harrier's Transference
Shield Harrier's Paralyzation

Scepter and focus for additional CC/pulls.

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Harrier's Transference
Focus Minstrel's Renewal

Shield is better used for tanking and focus is useful for pulling adds. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements.

 

Budget Options

  • Replace any of the sigil with  Superior Sigil of Water

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Magnanimous Maintenance Oil OR  Holographic Super Drumstick (budget alternative)

Build

Heal Quickness Build

[&DQctOhcqSTptAQ8BUx2GAYQdgQFrHWsBbR28AQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABWQAA]

Rotation and Utilities

There is no fixed rotation for this build. Even if you randomly press buttons you will still provide boons and adequate heals.

Quickness

You will need to use the following for quickness:

  1. Lively Lute
  2. Crescendo (while as many instruments are playing as possible)

Swiftness

  1. Mantra of Recovery
  2. Power Return

Your healing skills trigger  Relic of Febe to grant swiftness, use them off cooldown.

Shield or Focus can partially cover quickness, allowing you to drop  Relic of Febe

General rotation

  1. Generate Notes (Clones)
    • Journey (R2)
    • Phantasmal Sharpshooter (R4)
    • Mantra of Recovery -> Power Return (Heal)
    • Any tale with instrument-pairing
  2. Spend Notes to play Instruments as soon as they recharged (2 or more notes)
  3. Use Crescendo when you have 3 or more instruments playing
  4. You provide lots of boons and healing through instruments, rifle skills and utilities provide even more of them.

Utilty

You may replace skill slot 9 with any of the following:

  1. Tale of the Valiant Marshal for a stun break + short stability + additional notes
  2. Mantra of Concentration for permanent stability
  3. Mantra of Resolve for heavy condi cleanses
  4. Feedback for reflects
  5. Portal Entre for skips

Crowd Control

You have decent CC with your base kit:

  1. Phantasmal Sharpshooter (R4)
  2. Deafening Drum (F3)
  3. Tale of the August Queen (Elite)
  4. Crescendo (while Drum is playing)

Your weapon swaps allow access to additional CCs.

Optimisations

Mesmer as a class is designed to be flexible. The skill choices on this site are designed to be simple. There are an incredible amount of permutations that are not exhausted in this guide. You may introduce additional utilities when you are comfortable so play around.

entry.title: Boon Power Chronomancer [Boon DPS]
entry.content:
Notes

Power Boon Chronomancer uses the exact same rotation concept as Power Chronomancer. By simply swapping 1 trait, we are able to provide either full Quickness OR Alacrity uptime to our subgroup, while also contributing decent damage.

It provides quickness/alacrity through the use of Phantasms or Shatter skills, both of which are already incorporated into the base Power Chronomancer build.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Assassin's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Eagle
  • Food:  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak or  Bowl of Sweet and Spicy Butternut Squash Soup
  • Enhancement:  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQcBHQoaKAYjDwAAjwEAAM8SAACCAQAAsxIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACWgAvAAA=]

Select Stretched Time if you want to give Alacrity, or Seize the Moment if you want to give Quickness.

Rotation

Always start with:

Opener:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  4. Signet of the Ether
  5. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  6. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  7. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Signet of the Ether instantly recharges the cooldown of any Phantasmal type skill, and is used to deal more damage.

Use Well of Action off cooldown, as it activates Danger Time, giving you 10% more critical danage. Never delay Well of Action.

Your cooldowns will line up in such a way that you will always be able to pair Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) with either Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5) or Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2) off cooldown, pairing it with the accompanying skill that is off cooldown.

Every few rotations, your cooldowns will line up such that you will be able to cast the following combo:

Burst Combo:

  1. Well of Action
  2. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  3. Signet of the Ether
  4. Phantasmal Swordsman (Sword 5)
  5. Phantasmal Disenchanter
  6. Blurred Frenzy (Sword 2)

Which is basically your opener minus the first Phantasmal Disenchanter. Use your burst combo as soon as it is available.

On top of this, you should also use Split Second off cooldown, after the first cast. You should wait until you have 3 clones before casting Split Second for the first time.

Split Second has no cast point, can be casted at the same time as other skills, and will not interrupt any other skill you are currently casting.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are, in order of priority

CC Skills:

  1. Gravity Well (BIG CC)
  2. Illusionary Leap > Swap (Sword 3)
  3. Time Sink (more clones more CC)

In most situations, just using Gravity Well would be enough contribution to break the CC bar.

Advanced Tips

Distortion is also a very long immune skill, scaling up to a maximum of 3 seconds with the number of clones you currently have, and should be your panic button. It can also be used to dodge mechanics on various raids to increase your damage uptime.

 

You can also block attacks with Illusionary Riposte (Sword 4), but it is not recommended unless you are very confident of the mechanic. An example of using this to increase DPS would be on Deimos, where its cooldown is short enough to block both the knockback "pizza" mechanic and mindcrush, allowing you to maximise damage uptime on the boss.

 

Video

Changes

content/builds/boon-power-chronomancer.md was changed:

  • Vicious Expression: This trait now increases base strike damage and is further increased against boonless foes.

entry.title: Chrono Tank [aHeal/qHeal/Tank]
entry.content:
Assuming your party is experienced and only require a tank with multiple reliable blocks to ensure minimal risk of failure, majority of your actions will be based on the scepter auto-attack. The end of the auto-attack chain Ether Clone procs Illusionary Inspiration to provide heals and empower your shatter skills. Aegis and Stability are available on demand.

This build is has low physical complexity, has very few repetitions and is functional in instanced content, especially for new players wanting to volunteer for learning tank roles. You may find that the tanking ability is superior to other classes but relying on scepter alone without proper planning may result in mediocre healing. While this guide assumes no weapon swap is needed, to maximise its heal utility, you may consider swapping to rifle.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
Minstrel's Rebirth
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Minstrel's Concentration
Shield Minstrel's Transference
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Minstrel's
Amulet Minstrel's
Accessory Minstrel's
Accessory Minstrel's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Minstrel's
Ring Minstrel's
Relic Flock

Alternative

Consider swapping to rifle for additional healing

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Rifle Minstrel's Concentration
Transference

Consider swapping to focus for additional CC/pulls.

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Scepter Minstrel's Concentration
Focus Minstrel's Transference

Note: Rifle makes a better healer, but shield is better used for tanking. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements.

 

Budget Options

  • Replace  Superior Sigil of Transference with  Superior Sigil of Water

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Magnanimous Maintenance Oil

Build

Heal Quickness Build

[&DQctOhcqKC9tAQ8BkxKGAc8SgQGPAWsBsxK8AQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Swap to stretched time for Alacrity instead of Quickness

Rotation and Utilities

Firstly, do not panic. Chronomancer is an easy but brainy class to play. A lot of mechanics are invisible and running in the backend.

Opener

You may wish to adapt an opener into your rotation for initial quickness. Time warp is also useful for initial quickness.

The methods of executing your opener is non-exhaustive and you will find new ways that will suit your own playstyle. Many opener variations may include utilities but generally fulfil the following purpose

  1. Generate Alacrity/Quickness with minimal downtime.
  2. Stack might right at the start of the fight
  3. Provide essential boons required for the particular encounter.
  4. Provide boons that are missing from the subgroup.

An example opener would be

  1. Continuum Split F5
  2. Tides of Time (Shield 5)
  3. F1-F4 All your shatter skills
  4. Tides of Time (Shield 5)

The above is used due to easy execution, instant alacrity/quickness and early might, fury, protection, resistance, stability, regeneration and vigor.

Principles of generating Alacrity/Quickness

  1. Generate Clones using autos - Ether Clone.
  2. Use shatter skills when clones are full to heal allies and grant alacrity/quickness.
  3. Using Phantasm skills from your weapon skills and utilties also generate a significant amount of alacrity/quickness.
  1. These three steps are the core foundation of your build.
  2. Your last attack on the auto chain generates clones so you won't have enough quickness if you don't finish the auto chains.
  3. The rotation for quickness and alacrity is the same.
  4. You need to reserve backup when you can't generate clones or give boons. That's what the utilities are for.
  5. Phantasms generate more alacrity than clones, it will be good to bring at least 1-2 from weapon skills or utilities.

Maintenance Heals

  1. Clone generation provides a small but steady heal.
  2. Shattering clones provides a more sizeable heal.

Burst Heals

  1. Reserve the use of your wells if you can anticipate a large attack (eg. VG greens)
  2. Use your Mantra of Recovery as your emergency heal.[1]
  3. Your shatter skills provide significant heals even without clones.[2]
  4. Additional Mantras can be used in place of wells for very mobile fights.

Crowd Control

  1. You have decent CC by just using Tides of Time (Shield 5).
  2. Diversion (Shatter F3) provides a small CC.
  3. Signet of Humility (Elite) for a mega CC.
  4. Gravity Well (Elite) for a sizeable CC with other beneficial effects.
  5. Continuum Split can double cast some of your CC skills.

Tanking Rotation

  1. Cycle between your blocks when receiving heavy attacks
    • Illusionary Counter
    • Echo of Memory -> Deja Vu
    • Well of Precognition
  2. Other sources of group aegis can also be used but reserve it when it is absolutely required.

Other utilities and trait combos

  1. Bring Phantasmal Disenchanter / Arcane Thievery for boon strips.
  2. You may take Master of Manipulation for more/backup group aegis.
  3. Advanced strategies can be used with Mimic to duplicate skills for example:
    • Multi-feedback
    • Multi-blink
  4. Advanced strategies can be used with Continuum Split to duplicate skills for example:
    • Double Conjured Slash
    • Double Moa CC
  5. More advanced strategies can be used by combining Mimic and Continuum Split

Optimisation

Raid Ready

  1. W1 Vale Guardian - Use wells just before greens. This allows heals to come in right after green triggers. You can also use Well of Precognition to shield allies against teleport circles. Phantasmal Disenchanter and Arcane Thievery can strip boons off the Blue Guardian.
  2. W1 Gorseval - Start with initial stability which protects against the initial swipe. Subsequently, face the boss away from your group. Mantra of Concentration is also useful here.
  3. W1 Sabetha - You can teleport through flame wall with Blink. You have lots of CC options for Knuckles.
  4. W2 Slothasor - You need steady condition cleanses so spread the use of your shatters. Swapping to rifle is useful to heal players dropping poisons who are out of reach, bonus points if you can port them back to the group (coordination is difficult). Into the Void is often used to deal with slublings.
  5. W2 Matthias - Bring Feedback for reflects but if you are short of them, you can use Mimic to reset them. You have lots of CC options, but you may want to preserve Signet of Humility (Elite) when sacrifice mechanic is about to fail. The focus reflect options are too clunky to be reliable on this fight.
  6. W3 Escort - Most Chrono Tanks can swap to become a Tower Chrono. This is a niche build and not covered in this guide.
  7. W3 Twisted Castle - You may wish to attempt the button with the statues (left split). In this case after the double doors, use Echo of Memory immediately after entering and face them as they will throw hammers, then Blink to the next button. More skips can be done with an elementalist pair combo using Fiery Greatsword and clever use of portals and blink.
  8. W3 Xera - You are likely to tank this fight. This fight is mostly based on positioning. Into the Void (Focus Pull 4) is useful when tanking as it allows your team to cleave them more easily.
  9. W4 Cairn - Stability is required for skipping greens. Bring Well of Precognition and Mantra of Concentration.
  10. W4 Mursaat Overseer - Improved alacrity will allow you to claim tiles faster than other class. Using Continuum Split to duplicate Claim will allow you to claim tiles even faster.
  11. W4 Samarog - Try to bait as tank as much as possible. You can pre-cast your CC such as Tides of Time (Shield 5) before he pins you down (health bar indicator).
  12. W4 Deimos - Group Aegis is useful on CM. I prefer to Group Aegis before casting a Channeled block. You can also use manipulations for group aegis.
  13. W5 Soulless Horror - You will be tanking this fight and would definitely appreciate the added toughness. Bring Signet of Humility for this fight for the additional CC. Continuum Split has killed many Chrono Tanks in this fight so spatial awareness is important.
  14. W5 Dhuum - You will be tanking this fight and may need to do greens. If you do greens, Blink will be useful.
  15. W6 Conjured Amalgamate - You can CC multiple shields with Tides of Time (Shield 5) and Gravity Well. Conjured Slash can be duplicated with Continuum Split but you must click fast.
  16. W6 Twin Largos - This is a very mobile and difficult fight and you can mis-time your wells. However, Chronomancer is one of the best classes to take on either lanes.
  17. W6 Qadim - You have lots of CC options, some of which can be pre-casted for the mini bosses. You will need to tank one of the wyverns. Most of the time you will be bringing Portal Entre for the split phase and jumping puzzle.
  18. W7 Sabir - With clever use of Continuum Split, you can generate enough CC to break the bar during the 'coordinated' phase.
  19. W7 Adina - Tank on the grass. Just alternating your three blocks is enough. Feedback can be duplicated with Continuum Split and Mimic. For most groups, a 4th projectile block is not required but in case your options are exhausted, Phantasmal Warden. Temporal Curtain (Focus 4) may fail to block projectiles due to height limitations and you will need to sacrifice a trait.
  20. W7 QtP - You will be tanking this fight. Your timely channelled blocks are extrely useful to bypass attacks.
  21. W8 Greer - A reflection variant of this build makes this fight much easier.

  1. You can overheal using  Relic of the Flock. This generates barrier but has an ICD so you want to spread your casts. ↩

  2. Using all your shatters in panic without clones will destroy your boon uptime. ↩

entry.title: Heart of the Dragon [qDPS]
entry.content:
The easiest raid build, now with quickness!

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Dragon's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Force
Sword Berserker's Air
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Staff Berserker's Force
Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Thief

Alternatives: Full Berserker's and/or Scholar runes

Consumables

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQkPKgM2NBfcEdwRBhIGEisSKxLUEdQRyhHKEQIBAgAGEtQRKxIAAAAAAAADawA1AAUAAA==]

Notes

Use:

  1. Impossible Odds while in Legendary Assassin Stance
  2. Facet of Darkness, Facet of Chaos, and Facet of Strength in Legendary Dragon Stance. Use Facet of Strength again (it should now be Burst of Strength) to consume it.

Swap legends off cooldown in Legendary Assassin Stance, and swap legends once you have use Burst of Strength twice in Legendary Dragon Stance

Use off cooldown:

  1. Deathstrike (Sword 5)
  2. Shackling Wave (Sword 4)
  3. Chilling Isolation (Sword 2)

Following the above, you should never run out of energy, unless you have to CC.

Crowd Control

You should always swap to your Staff for CC. Part of your job as a boon DPS is to do everything possible to get the most damage out of your DPS players, and this includes giving them boons and CCing so they don't have to.

Your CC skills are:

  1. Surge of the Mists (Staff 5)
  2. Chaotic Release ( Facet of Chaos flipped skill) in Legendary Dragon Stance
  3. Jade Winds (in Legendary Assassin Stance)

Video

entry.title: Dread Apothecary [qDPS]
entry.content:
My pain brings you strength!

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Assassin's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
Berserker's Dragonhunter
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Greatsword Berserker's Force
Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Thief

Alternatives: Full Berserker's and/or Scholar runes

Consumables

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQg1KTIlQCnnGpsA7BrkAG8BdgB1AXAB6BqVAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACMgBaAAA=]

Notes

Quickness Harbinger provides boons, and most importantly quickness, in 2 ways, through Elixirs and by Harbinger Shroud. Since you cannot use Elixirs while you are in shroud, and you want to be in Harbinger Shroud as much as possible to both give boons and do damage, there is a certain minimum rotation speed you are aiming to hit to be able to give 100% quickness. While it is quite forgiving to be able to hit this minimum speed, some practice is still required on the class to ensure full uptime.

Start by using Elixir of Promise, Elixir of Anguish, and Elixir of Ambition to build up blight stacks. Elixir of Anguish and Elixir of Ambition also gives quickness, among other boons, and you should take care not to use them too early before the boon strip. You should always start with these 3 skills after any prolonged phases of not attacking.

The rotation consists of 3 main combos:

  1. Pre Shroud
  2. In Shroud
  3. Out Shroud

And should be looped in this order indefinitely.

Pre Shroud combo consists of:

  1. Grasping Darkness (Greatsword 5)
  2. Nightfall (Greatsword 4)
  3. Harbinger Shroud

In Shroud combo consists of:

  1. Devouring Cut (Shroud 3)
  2. Dark Barrage (Shroud 2)
  3. Do it 3 times
  4. Exit Harbinger Shroud

Basically Shroud 3 Devouring Cut has a very long after cast and you are using Shroud 2 Dark Barrage to cancel the after cast.

Out Shroud combo consists of:

  1. Gravedigger (Greatsword 2)
  2. Death Spiral (Greatsword 3)
  3. Auto Attack Chain
  4. Gravedigger (Greatsword 2)

You will know when your auto attack chain is completed when the cooldown of Gravedigger (Greatsword 2) resets, in which case you should use it again immediately.

On top of this, you want to use:

  1. Elixir of Promise
  2. Elixir of Anguish
  3. Elixir of Ambition

off cooldown. These skills are also very easy to cancel and you should try your best not to cancel them by pressing the next skill too quickly.

Below 50% boss HP, you will notice that Gravedigger (Greatsword 2) has no cooldown. You should use it as much as possible and Out Shroud combo basically becomes spam Gravedigger (Greatsword 2) until Enter Shroud is off cooldown.

If you find yourself being forced out of Shroud early, just carry on and do Out Shroud combo repeatedly until Grasping Darkness (Greatsword 5) is off cooldown, then recover and continue the rotation.

Video

Build demo in "high" level play

Changes

content/builds/quickness-power-harbinger.md was changed:

  • Spiteful Talisman: This trait no longer reduces the cooldowns of axe and focus skills. This trait now increases base strike damage and is further increased against boonless foes.
  • Deathly Haste: This trait has been reworked. Grant boons to nearby allies when you enter Harbinger Shroud and when you use Dark Barrage.

entry.title: Unload Quickeye [qDPS]
entry.content:
The purpose of writing this guide is to provide a very low intensity method to play PvE deadeye while providing quickness. This build is low cognition, has low physical complexity, and has very few repetitions. This build is functional in both open world and instanced content. This build also provides abundance of Might and Fury boons that support healers that are lacking in effort.

This simplified build also allows you to perform niche roles like pylon kiting, which is extremely useful when learning the role and not wanting to be bogged down by rotation. I have completed all 8 wings in NM with this build being fairly high/average ranked in DPS meters. The improvised quickness variant uses the same equipment as the Unload Deadeye version and is able to overcap on quickness through a very simple rotation and minimal use of cantrips.

Gearing and Consumables

Equipment

  • Full Berserker's
  • Dragon’s if desired as the build is heavily overcapped on precision
  • Dual Pistols

Runes

  •  Superior Rune of the Scholar OR
  •  Superior Rune of the Dragonhunter

Sigils

  •  Superior Sigil of Force
  •  Superior Sigil of Impact
  •  Superior Sigil of Air(Budget Alternative)

Relic

  •  Relic of the Thief

Food

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Plate of Spicy Moa Wings

Utility

  •  Superior Sharpening Stone(Power)

Build

Quickness DPS

[&DQUjHxw9Oj8MAQwBIBdYATMBMwGsFlYBKAAoAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACNgBZAAA=]
  • Note: Using Improvisation increases the quickness uptime and makes the build easier to play at a cost of slightly reduced DPS
  • You should prioritise quickness uptime instead of DPS as this affects the entire subgroup.
  • It is okay to overcap on precision due to the occasional need to switch to Practiced Tolerance

Rotation and Utilities

DPS Rotation

Precasts - Thieves Guild (use on refresh), Hide in Shadows

  1. Set Unload (Pistols 3) to auto. Use Assassin's Signet for a small DPS boost on cooldown.

Quickness Rotation

  1. For the quickness, you need to use Steal Time. Deadeye's Mark provides one charge.
  2. Mercy as a cantrip provides 1 charge of Steal Time and resets Deadeye's Mark.
  3. Your other cantrip(s) also provide 1 charge of Steal Time
  4. You may reserve Steal Time at full malice to gain stealth/Revealed and a damage boost through Revealed Training
  5. If you mess up your rotation, your group will lose quickness, for example
    • using Mercy when Deadeye's Mark is about to refresh
    • using Deadeye's Mark before using the charge from Mercy
    • being split up from your group due to mechanics
  6. Consider taking improvisation if you find quickness difficult to upkeep.

Quickness Rotation with Improvisation

Improvisation, One in the Chamber and Fire for Effect paired with Mercy provides 30s of Quickness right at the start of the fight.

  1. For the quickness, you need to use Steal Time. Each steal provides two charges.
  2. Mercy as a cantrip provides 1 charge of Steal Time and resets Deadeye's Mark. Use Deadeye's Mark which you can use immediately for two more charges of Steal Time.
  3. There is so much quickness overcap with improvisation it is almost impossible to have any downtime.

Crowd Control rotation

  1. You have decent CC by just spamming Head Shot (Pistol 4).
  2. You can take Basilisk Venom (elite) for a minimal DPS loss if you still need more CC.

Sustainability

  1. Your heal skill Signet of Malice should provide enough heals per hit.
  2. You can opt to take Invigorating Precision but this is not an efficient choice for sustainability.
  3.  Relic of Zakiros (eg. Kiting)

Optimisation

Maximising Cleave/Pierce

This build may underperform at high end raiding but it can do extremely well in fights where ranged pierce is able to double your DPS. Some targets are tall and piercing should not be presumed as your rounds may fire upwards (refer to the benchmark video shot sideways). The effect causes you to miss all posterior targets. Instead, you need to manually select the furthest target in order to hit as many targets as possible. Ranged pierce can also be done with a rifle but this is not covered in this low-intensity guide.

Niche Roles

  1. W1 Sabetha - Both the quickness and DPS builds are ideal to do mechanics as you can carry a shadowstep and overcap boons, sacrificing very little for doing cannons.
  2. W3 Xera - This build is able to function at range decently without stacking, making it ideal to step on buttons during the 3-button split phase
  3. W6 Qadim - While solo lamp is ideally performed using daredevil (same equipment), you may choose to use this build instead if 2 players are sent to do Lamp.
  4. W7 QtP - This build is able to pylon kite. A video is provided for reference.

Videos

QTP Pylon Kiting

Latest patch changes

Deadeye's Mark has no cast time and no longer interrupts your rotation. The increase in DPS is minimal.
